context: Scale orchestrator from 25 to 50 concurrent agents; eliminate contention bottlenecks
assumptions: Completion record writes are serialized or locked (contention expected)
Git worktree creation is synchronous per agent (latency accumulates)
Brief generation is not memoized or is regenerated per spawn

optimizations.title: Shard Completion Records (JSONL per agent or batch)
optimizations.bottleneck: Concurrent JSON writes to shared completion store
implementation: Append-only JSONL format (atomic per line, no locking)
Merge shards at validation time via sorted merge (deterministic)
Preserve existing head.json API (lazy-load merged state on read)
metrics: Orchestrator dispatch latency → reduced by eliminated serialization
optimizations.validation: Validate merged completion records match sequential write semantics; test 50-agent batch completion

optimizations.title: Batch Git Worktree Creation (Parallel or Pre-allocated)
optimizations.bottleneck: O(n) synchronous disk I/O per worktree add
optimizations.riskLevel: Medium (concurrent git requires care; test thoroughly)
implementation: Use GNU parallel or Node worker threads to spawn `git worktree add` in parallel (batch size: 5-10)
Orchestrator batches agent spawns: dispatch 5 agents, wait for worktrees, dispatch next 5
Alternative: pre-allocate pool of 50 empty worktrees on startup (5 min init, then instant assignment)
metrics: Agent startup latency → reduced by ~300-500ms per batch
optimizations.validation: Ensure git operations are atomic (no corruption); test concurrent worktree creation with shared repo

optimizations.title: Memoize Brief Generation (LRU or Precompute)
optimizations.bottleneck: Repeated nodeSpec → Brief conversion per agent spawn
optimizations.expectedROI: 0.5-1x speedup (dispatch latency only, not critical path)
implementation: Option A: Precompute all briefs at DAG load (load-time cost, zero runtime cost)
Option B: LRU cache with max 100 entries, keyed by nodeId (lazy, higher latency bound)
Option C: Mem cache in orchestrator process, keyed by (nodeId, agentId, iteration)
optimizations.validation: Confirm no stale briefs on DAG reload; test cache hit rate in 50-agent scenario

optimizations.title: Buffer Agent Metrics (Batch Flush vs Per-Agent Write)
optimizations.bottleneck: Concurrent metrics writes (if collected per agent finish)
implementation: Collect @pre and @post metrics in agent process (in-memory buffer)
Flush to `.roadmap/metrics.jsonl` in batch (collect 10 metrics, write once)
Or: send metrics to memory buffer in orchestrator, batch flush every 10s
optimizations.validation: Ensure no metrics loss on process crash; test 50-agent metric collection

optimizations.title: Colocate Orchestrator and Agents (If Distributed)
optimizations.bottleneck: Network I/O if agents are remote
optimizations.expectedROI: 5-10x latency improvement (if remote; zero if local)
implementation: Confirm orchestrator and agents are on same machine (check architecture)
If distributed: consolidate to single machine OR batch brief delivery (5 briefs per request)
Use local socket or in-process dispatch for same-machine agents
optimizations.validation: Measure network latency if agents are remote; refactor only if latency > 5% of total

phaseRollout.rationale: Top 2 bottlenecks, parallelizable implementation (separate PRs), critical path impact
phaseRollout.successCriteria: 50 agents scale to 2-2.5x of 25-agent time (linear or near-linear)

phaseRollout.rationale: Orthogonal optimizations, lower priority, implement if metrics show dispatch overhead > 10%
Only if empirical profiling shows network I/O > 5% of total time
phaseRollout.successCriteria: Colocated orchestrator and agents (verify via lsof or process inspection)

empiricalValidation.postOptimization: Re-profile 50 agents after each phase rollout; confirm scaling improvement
stopConditions: Linear scaling achieved (50 agents ≤ 2.2x of 25-agent time)
Further optimization ROI < 10% improvement per day of effort

risks.risk: Concurrent git operations corrupt repository state
risks.mitigation: Test worktree-batch with git fsck; isolate worktree operations to independent repos if needed

risks.risk: Completion record merge loses atomicity during validation
risks.mitigation: Validate merged records are idempotent and deterministic (sorted merge); test with synthetic race conditions

risks.risk: Metrics buffering loses data on orchestrator crash
risks.mitigation: Buffer to disk immediately (JSONL append), not just memory; flush on graceful shutdown
